name: TA-65 health-maintenance program reported immune and short-telomere changes but was uncontrolled
summary: An early human program analysis is supportive context but too confounded to drive the track rating.
statement: A commercial health-maintenance program including TA-65 reported reduced proportions of senescent immune cells and fewer very short leukocyte telomeres, but attribution to TA-65 is uncertain.
population_or_model: Adults enrolled in a commercial health-maintenance program
quantitative_note: The abstract reports declines in senescent CD8+/CD28- T cells at 6, 9, and 12 months and a reduced percentage of short leukocyte telomeres under 4 kbp.
caveats: The program combined TA-65, a supplement pack, counseling, and repeated laboratory monitoring.
The analysis was not a randomized placebo-controlled test of TA-65.
Commercial context and confounding limit interpretation.
